questionText: The random variable $Z$ has the standard normal distribution, $Z \sim N(0, 1)$. Find $P(-1 < Z < 1)$, giving your answer to $4$ significant figures.

steps.explanation: The standard normal curve is symmetric about $0$, and $\Phi(z) = P(Z < z)$ is the area to the left of $z$.

steps.description: Write the interval as a difference of areas
steps.explanation: The area of a central strip is the area to the left of the upper end minus the area to the left of the lower end.
By symmetry the area to the left of $-1$ equals the area to the right of $1$, which is $1 - \Phi(1)$. This lets us use a single table value.
Substituting removes the negative $z$ so everything is expressed through $\Phi(1)$, giving the neat formula $2\Phi(1) - 1$.
With $\Phi(1) = 0.8413$ the arithmetic gives the central area.
About $68\%$ of the distribution lies within one standard deviation of the mean, which is exactly the first part of the $68$–$95$–$99.7$ rule.
